observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation for that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure disclosure of the necessary information in the NTFS. This disclosure is crucial for aiding users in comprehending the financial statements and enhancing their informational value.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken for this recommendation.
observations.reason: NTFS were still not properly disclosed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the proper disclosure of the road network system in the NTFS.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken for this recommendation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineering Office failed to provide the needed information relative to Road Network disclosures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the OIC-Municipal General Services Officer to: a. Furnish the Audit Team with copies of POs with supporting documents within five working days from the issuance and/or execution; and b. Make sure that POs contain all the basic data or information as prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-010.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken for this recommendation. Prepared POs contained all the basic data or information.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous documents coupled with multiple signatories, there were delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Chairman, Bids and Awards Committee to: a. Furnish the Audit Team with copies of approved contract with supporting documents within five working days from the issuance and/or execution; and b. Comply strictly with the requirement of COA Circular Nos. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken for this recommendation. The Bids and Awards Committee Chairperson assured compliance with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous documents coupled with multiple signatories, the prescribed time for the submission of the executed contract was not met.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to remit the barangay’s share from RPT and CTC collections within five days after the end of each quarter as mandated under Section 271 (d) of LGC.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken for this recommendation. The assigned handler in the transmittal of barangay’s quarterly RPT and CTC share for CY 2024 was on sick leave for more than two months and the Municipal Accountant was hospitalized and was not able to orient other staff to handle such task. The submission of the report of collection and remittance of barangays were delayed.
observations.reason: Due to late closing of the monthly accounting reports and staff unavailability (sick leave, hospitalization) causing delays in transmittal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal General Services Officer to:
a. Exert extra effort in identifying the cause of unreconciled discrepancies of
P198,983,633.83 by conducting thorough reconciliation of records; and
b. Consider the opportunity to cleanse its PPE account balances by availing the
one-time cleansing activities in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to
update the RPCPPE by excluding the 2,781 small value items and completing the
details of 539 PPE items without recorded unit values.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts to recognize
the collections of RPT and SET for CY 2024 totaling P3,364,197.81 to reflect the
accurate balances of the affected accounts; and
b. Observe the proper entries in the collection and recognition of income for RPT
and SET as prescribed by the pertinent provisions of MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
• Effect the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts to recognize
as expense the noted issued inventories; and
• Ensure that further issuance of inventories is recognize immediately as
expense in accordance with the pertinent provisions of MNGAS for LGUs.
b. Municipal General Services Officer to provide the Municipal Accounting Office
with inventory records to serve as basis of the Municipal Accountant in the
recognition of expense portion of the inventories and for reconciliation
purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to trace the remaining net variance of P74,438.63
between the balance of cash per subsidiary ledgers and cashbooks and draw
the necessary adjustments in their records to come up with reconciled account
balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reclassify the
construction of other structures and the procurement of native pigs erroneously
recorded as Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses to Other Structures
and Breeding Stocks accounts, respectively; and
b. Ensure that disbursements are properly recorded under the appropriate
accounts, as prescribed under the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2015-009
and 2024-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Demand the immediate liquidation of the outstanding CAs totaling P200,646.00;
b. Submit immediately the noted lacking documents in the granting and liquidation
of CAs to prevent the suspension of the transactions; and
c. Ensure that CAs are fully liquidated at year end and liquidated properly with
complete supporting documents within the prescribed period in accordance
with the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and
2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging the salaries and wages,
allowances and bonuses of the noted regular, casual and JO personnel against
the PREP; instead, charge the expenses thereof against the appropriate offices’
budget in which they are actually detailed; and
b. PREP Supervising Manager, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to
develop measures that will improve the ability of the PREP to generate adequate
income to sustain its operations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, in
coordination with the Municipal Human Resources Management Office, to:
a. Submit immediately the noted lacking documents to prevent the issuance of
Notice of Suspension on the transactions; and
b. Ensure that all claims for terminal leave benefits are paid based on duly
processed DVs with complete supporting documents, in compliance with
Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Section 5.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee
Chairperson, to immediately submit the noted certifications from FDA and
manufacturers to the Office of the Auditor to avoid suspension of the
transactions; and
b. Bids and Awards Committee Chairperson to ensure that participating suppliers
of drugs and medicines are equipped with the necessary certifications required
by Section 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee Chairperson to furnish the Audit Team with copies
of the approved contract agreement and its supporting documents within five
working days from the issuance and/or execution in compliance with Section
3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and
b. General Services Officer to strictly comply with Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular
No. 2009-001 and Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 in the submission of
POs and its supporting documents within the prescribed period of five working
days from its issuance and the Notice of Deliveries within 24 hours after
acceptance of delivery to the Office of the Auditor to prevent undue delay in the
review thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, in
coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Human Resource
Management Office, to implement stringent measures in the payroll preparation to
ensure that the approval of loan applications of personnel should not cause their
net take home pay to be lower than the mandatory limit of P5,000.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Consider designating Municipal Human Resource and Management Officer that will
manage all aspects of employment process, including recruitment, training, employee
relations, and ensuring adherence to CSC laws, rules and regulations; and
b. Direct the Municipal Administrator, in coordination with the Municipal Human
Resource Management Office, to execute contracts between the Municipality
and the JO personnel specifying their defined functions and duration of service
in accordance with the pertinent provisions of CSC Resolution No. 02-0790 and
COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to: a. Provide a complete information on the RPCPPE; and b. Consolidate the RPCPPE of all the offices for accurate assessment.
We likewise reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the:
a. ICO-Municipal General Services Officer to maintain Property Cards for each class of PPE including the description, acquisition, transfer, disposal, and other information about the PPE owned by the municipality for control purposes;
b. Municipal Accountant to update/maintain her subsidiary ledger cards to provide adequate information for each category of PPE; and
c. ICO-Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs are recorded in their respective property records and reconcile the same upon availing the one-time rendition of RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction:
No action was taken for this recommendation. RPCPPE was prepared, however, it did not provide complete information and still not reconciled with the accounting records.
The Municipality has prepared a consolidated RPCPPE for CY 2024.
The Municipal Accountant is now maintaining acquired in CY 2023 subsidiary ledger cards for PPE still not prepared and maintained.
observations.reason:
RPCPPE was prepared, however, it did not provide complete information and still not reconciled with the accounting records.
There was still an unreconciled variances between inventory records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible as the basis of the latter in setting-up RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction:
The assigned IT Focal was instructed to prepare the updated list of taxpayers by December 2024. A journal entry voucher was prepared in October 2024 to correct the setup of RPT and SET Receivables.
Moving forward, instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.reason: The assigned IT Focal was instructed to prepare the updated list of taxpayers by December 2024. A journal entry voucher was prepared in October 2024 to correct the setup of RPT and SET Receivables.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to update, review and compare her cashbook entries with the accounting records and effect adjustments for the reconciling items for fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The unreconciled net discrepancy has been decreased by P74,438.63 as of year-end. The Municipal Treasurer is now providing the list of unreleased checks regularly in order to make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts. The Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer are in the process of reconciling their variances. Journal Entry Voucher No. 300-2025-04-001 dated April 30, 2025 was drawn to correct the variance in the Cash - Local Treasury - TF account.
observations.reason:
Reiterated with modification in Audit Observation No. 4, Page Nos. 32-34, Part II of this Report.
Despite decrease, there remained unreconciled differences between subsidiary ledgers and cashbooks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to submit at year-end Schedule of Unreleased Checks to the Municipal Accountant for adjustment in the books for fair presentation of affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasury Office is now providing the list of unreleased checks regularly in order to make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: • Municipal Accountant to prepare accounting entries to take up the cancellation of stale check in the books of accounts of the Municipality by debiting CIB-LCCA and crediting Accounts Payable in the amount of P25,436.22. • Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare and send written notice to the payees at least one month before a check becomes stale as required under Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The noted staled checks were already cancelled and adjusted in the books of accounts. The Municipal Treasurer is now ensuring that checks are regularly monitored and that prompt preparation of notice to payees of unclaimed checks before it become stale is made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate timely submission of the DVs and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The submission of the DVs is continuously improved. The DVs are still being submitted to the COA on a late status, based on our controls there was a slight improvement. The accounting staff handling the scanning of paid DVs and some checks were still not claimed by payees. The Municipal Accountant reminded staff of the deadlines in order not to hamper the Audit Team’s timelines.
observations.reason: The DVs were still submitted late; awaiting full transmittal of paid checks and DVs from the Municipal Treasury Office in order to submit to the Office of the Auditor the 100% paid DVs and its supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and other relevant officials to initiate the automation of wage payment of JO workers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has complied. The Municipal Treasury Office had coordinated with the Municipal Human Resources Management Office for the enrolment of JO workers in the payroll system by securing automated teller machine cards with LBP.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasury Office coordinated with relevant officials to initiate automation; LBP ATM cards were being secured for JO workers.

observations.recommendation: We likewise reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. ICO-MGSO to maintain Property Cards for each class of PPE indicating the description, acquisition, transfer, disposal, and other information about the PPE owned by the Municipality for control purposes; b. Municipal Accountant to update subsidiary ledger cards that provide adequate information for each category of PPE; and c. ICO-MGSO and the Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation of property records to assure the existence, completeness, and accuracy of reported PPE balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: The RPCPPE was not complete in relevant information necessary for a proper review, consolidated RPCPPE was prepared.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. OIC – Municipal Treasurer to provide the Certified List of all Real Property Tax Delinquencies for CY 2022 reported by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office; and b. Municipal Accountant to draw JEV to set up RPT Receivables/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; Moving forward, instruct the OIC – Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Certified List of all Real Property Tax Delinquencies for CY 2023 was used as basis for updating the RPT and SET Receivables. The Municipal Accountant drew a Journal Entry Voucher to increase the RPT and SET Receivables by P448,727.78 each.
observations.reason: A different basis was used to set up the RPT and SET receivables.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required information in the NTFS to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with the additional information value.
observations.managementAction: Disclosures were made in some account.
observations.reason: Some account in the financial statements were inadvertently not provided with proper disclosure.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to update, review and compare her cashbook entries with the accounting records and effect adjustments for the reconciling items for fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of cashbook and book of accounts was started and still on going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to submit at year-end Schedule of Unreleased Checks to the Municipal Accountant for adjustment in the book for fair presentation of affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: The list was provided only on April 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the proper disclosure of the road network system in the NTFS.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: The format prescribed by the COA Circular was not used.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate timely submission of the Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: Lack of sufficient manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the OIC – Municipal General Services Officer to: a. Furnish the Audit Team with copies of Purchase Orders with supporting documents within five working days from the issuance and/or execution; and b. Make sure that purchase orders contain all the basic data or information as prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-010.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: They submitted the PO together with the DVs.

Moving forward, instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and ICO – Municipal

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor’s Office, direct the OIC – Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant a copy of the Certified List of all Real Property Tax Delinquencies. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and OIC – Municipal Treasurer to analyze and investigate the cause/s of the deficiency noted between the balance of the RPT Receivable and the Certified List of all Real Property Delinquent Taxes as of December 31, 2022 and make the necessary adjustments in their records, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: A reconciliation was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prescribe minimum monthly net take home pay under the GAA be strictly observed.
observations.managementAction: The management made an adjustment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. stop the practice of granting the RATA in the first half of the month without any proof of actual rendition of service to the payroll; and b. submit certification to the Audit Team to determine the propriety of claims of Transportation Officials/Department Heads.
observations.managementAction: The management made an adjustment. Certification were submitted together with the DVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Office, to optimally utilize the 20 percent DF to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Due to budgetary constraints funds for a particular project was budgeted each year before they are implemented the following year. This allows the municipality to provide a huge impact with budget allotted for two or more years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with MDRRMO, ensure that the utilization of the LDRRMF is strictly in compliance with the pertinent provision of RA No. 10121, Item 5 of NDRRMC–DBM–DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and Item 4.3 of NDRRMC–DBM–DILG–CSC JMC No. 2014-01.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMF was utilized strictly according to the regulations.

The Municipal Accountant commented that their office did not receive certified list of taxpayers from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office at the beginning of the year, the reason why she cannot make the set-up of RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3,
Page No. 32.

observations.recommendation:

a. Update, review and compare her cashbook entries with the accounting records and effect adjustments for the reconciling items.
b. Reconcile her cashbook with the accounting records at least quarterly to avoid the accumulation of discrepancies.
c. Maintain a cashbook for the DBP accounts.
observations.managementAction: The OIC – Municipal Treasurer assured compliance with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 4,
Page No. 33.
Discrepancy of P6,429,254.60 in LBP CIB accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. evaluate the validity and propriety of dormant payables, which have been outstanding for more than two years, to determine/confirm whether there are actual claimants and these dormant payables are supported with proper documentation; and
b. seek authority from the Sanggunian Bayan for the reversion of dormant payables, except unliquidated balances of Accounts Payable in the Trust Fund, to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund pursuant to Section 98 PD. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that they are in the process of identifying long outstanding liability accounts that can be treated as part of Unappropriated Surplus particularly the Due to LGUs and Other Payables accounts that were no longer for liquidation with the source agencies. She also informed the Audit Team that she will provide the list of dormant payables to the Sangguniang Bayan for proper resolution.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 6,
Page No. 36.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to set up and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of operating performance and viability of the Municipality's economic enterprises and 20 percent Development Fund, and to serve as a decision-making tool of the agency. Also, record all transactions about these economic enterprises to the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that she will provide the necessary separate financial reports for the Economic Enterprises.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 10,
Page No. 43.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor directs the Inventory
Committee in coordination with the OIC – GSO to:
a. conduct a comprehensive complete physical inventory count of all PPEs of the
Municipality yearly to determine their actual existence and condition; and
b. prepare the RPCPPE which should incorporate the results of the physical count
and submit the same to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year.

We likewise reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. OIC – GSO to maintain Property Cards for each class of PPE indicating the
description, acquisition, transfer, disposal, and other information about the
PPE owned by the Municipality for control purposes;
b. Municipal Accountant to update subsidiary ledger cards that provide adequate
information for each category of PPE; and
c. OIC – GSO and the Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation of
property records to assure the existence, completeness, and accuracy of
reported PPE balances in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to disclose the required information in the Notes to Financial
Statements to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to
provide them with the additional information value.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. OIC – Municipal Treasurer to maintain a Real Property Tax Account
Register/Taxpayer’s index card as the basis for the certified list of taxpayers;

b. OIC – Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the
certified list showing the name of taxpayers, and the amount due and
collectible as the basis of the latter in setting-up Real Property Tax Receivables
and Special Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year for CY
2021;

c. Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher to set up Real Property
Tax Receivables/Special Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the
year;

d. OIC – Municipal Treasurer to come up with a certified list of taxpayers with the
uncollected amount of RPT and SET Receivables from CYs 2002-2020, and
submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording/reconciliation; and

e. Municipal Accountant to reconciled her records with the records of the OIC –
Municipal Treasurer and make the necessary adjustment, if necessary.

Moving forward, instruct the OIC – Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
to strictly comply with Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC – Municipal Treasurer
to:

a. Update, review and compare her cashbook entries with the accounting records
and effect adjustments for the reconciling items;

b. Reconcile her cashbook with the accounting records at least quarterly to avoid
the accumulation of discrepancies; and

c. Maintain a cashbook for the DBP accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor liquidate his cash advance and require
the Sanggunian Pambayan member Roberto Flores to liquidate their respective
cash advances or require the refund of the same in case of non-liquidation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to monitor the liquidation of cash advances and ensure that all cash advances are
fully liquidated at the end of the year.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. evaluate the validity and propriety of dormant payables, which have been
outstanding for more than two years, to determine/confirm whether there are
actual claimants and these dormant payables are supported with proper
documentation; and

b. seek authority from the Sanggunian Bayan for the reversion of dormant
payables, except unliquidated balances of Accounts Payable in the Trust Fund,
to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund pursuant to Section 98 PD.
1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
identify the cause of the variance noted in principal and interest paid in the
amount of P138,160.47 and P508,859.72, respectively with the Land Bank of the
Philippines and accrue the interest charges of the existing loans to present the
correct interest expenses of the borrowings for the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Municipal Assessor to exert more efforts to retrieve all the required documents
and information for the land titling and refer the matter to the concerned
agencies to facilitate the processing of titles; and

b. OIC – Municipal General Services Officer to initiate the transfer and titling of the
aforementioned parcels of land in the name of Municipality to protect its
interest from third party claims and encroachment in compliance with Section
39 (2) of PD No. 1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. OIC – GSO to expedite the preparation and submission of the list of all
insurable property and ensure that it is all is covered by property insurance
with the Property Insurance Fund of the GSIS as required under RA No. 656 and
COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992; and

b. Municipal Budget Officer to include in the annual budget the amount necessary
for the insurance premiums of all insurable property.

enterprises and 20 percent Development Fund, and to serve as a decision-making
tool of the agency. Also, record all transactions about these economic enterprises
to the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to create:
a. a Physical Inventory-taking Team who will be tasked to conduct a physical
inventory of all owned/controlled PPEs of the Municipality; and
b. an Inventory Committee who will reconcile the results of inventory with the
property and accounting records.

We further recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
i. maintain and/or update a subsidiary ledger for each parcel of land;
ii. record the cost directly attributable to the acquisition of land as part of
the acquisition cost and not as a separate land item;
iii. coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Assessor for the reconciliation
of recorded land in the books of accounts with that of the record of the
Office of the Municipal Assessor;
iv. request the Appraisal Committee for the fair value of the unrecorded
parcel of land, as a result of reconciliation with the record of the Office of
the Municipal Assessor;
v. submit a list of untitled parcels of land to the Municipal Mayor for
consideration and further action;
vi. maintain and/or update subsidiary ledger card for each category of PPE;
vii. coordinate with the OIC-Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) for the
reconciliation of recorded motor vehicle in the books of accounts with
31
the record of the OIC-Municipal General Services Officer;
viii. request the Appraisal Committee for the fair value of the unrecorded
motor vehicle, as a result of reconciliation with the record of the OIC-
Municipal General Services Officer; and
ix. prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor depreciation schedule,
make the necessary adjustment to provide a correct carrying value of the
PPE, and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for review and
evaluation.
b. Office of the Municipal Assessor to:
i. cooperate with the Municipal Accountant in the reconciliation of recorded
land in the books of accounts with the record of the office; and
ii. make necessary updating in the record of the office based on the
reconciliation;
c. OIC- MGSO to:
i. maintain a Property Card to record the description, acquisition, transfer,
disposal, and other information about the PPE. It shall be kept for each
class of PPE;
ii. cooperate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of
recorded motor vehicle in the books of accounts with his record;
iii. make the necessary updating or correction of his record when necessary
based on the reconciliation;
iv. initiate disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles; and
v. update the ARE using the Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR)
template, which is now the document used for the issuance of PPE and
the acknowledgment of the end-user.
d. Inventory Committee to render the RCPPE and submit the same to the auditor
concerned not later than January 31 of each year; and
e. Municipal Accountant, OIC – Municipal General Services Officer and the Office
of the Municipal Assessor to conduct periodic reconciliation of property
records to assure the completeness and accuracy of reported PPE balances in
the financial statements.

We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. designate a person-in-charge for the titling of the untitled parcels of land found
as a result of reconciliation between the accounting records and the
assessor‟s record; and
b. designate a person-in-charge for the registration of owned/donated and
unrecorded but road-worthy motor vehicles in the name of the Municipal
Government with the Land Transportation Office.

We further recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. determine the accountable official for deceased livestock and other missing
properties recorded as biological assets and have the accountable officer
submit a Notice of Loss to the COA of Auditor concerned and the Accounting
Office;
b. advice the accountable officer to request/apply for relief from accountability on
32
the deceased livestock and other missing properties in accordance with COA
Memorandum No. 92-751, Documentation on Petitions/Requests for Relief
from Accountability, and for the fair presentation of the account in the
financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1;
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the Notice of Loss, to draw
a Journal Entry Voucher to recognize the accountability of the accountable
officer by debiting Due to Officer and Employees account and crediting the
particular biological assets; and by debiting Loss of Assets account and
crediting the account Due to Officers and Employees, in case the request was
granted;
d. instruct the Municipal Accountant to consider setting up impairment loss to
allow the presentation of the Biological Asset account in its fair value; and
e. instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to remit the proceeds of the sale of the
livestock to the Municipal Treasurer‟s Office and request issuance of the
corresponding receipt in the name of the buyer of the livestock sold.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
present the required significant accounting policies in the Notes to F/S to assist the
users to better interpret the financial statements.

We further reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor directs the
Municipal Accountant to disclose the required information in the Notes to F/S to
assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with
the additional information value.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor vigorously direct the:
a. Municipal Engineer to provide the ICO-MGSO and the Municipal Accountant
with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for
road projects; and
b. ICO-MGSO to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards;
and
c. Municipal Accountant to disclose the Road Networks in the Notes to F/S based
on documents provided by the Municipal Engineer containing complete
information of road projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to maintain a Real Property Tax Account
Register/Taxpayer’s index card as the basis for the certified list of taxpayers;
b. Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the certified list
showing the name of taxpayers, and the amount due and collectible as the
basis of the latter in setting-up Real Property Tax Receivables and Special
Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year for CY 2020; and
c. Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher to set up Real Property
Tax Receivables/Special Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the
year;
d. Municipal Treasurer to come up with a certified list of taxpayers with the
uncollected amount of RPT and SET Receivables from CYs 2002-2019, and
submit the same to the ICO – Municipal Accountant for
recording/reconciliation; and
e. Municipal Accountant to reconciled her records with the records of the
Municipal Treasurer and make the necessary adjustment, if necessary
Moving forward, instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly
comply with Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other and regularly reconcile their
respective records of cash balances for timely adjustment of discrepancies/errors for
accurate and fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. set up an Allowance for Impairment loss for dormant accounts pursuant to
paragraph 72 of IPSAS 29 in order to present fairly the receivable account in
the F/S; and
b. provide the necessary supporting documents for the request for write-off duly
in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 and submit to the Auditor for
review and evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. evaluate the validity and propriety of dormant payables, which has been
outstanding for more than two years, to determine/confirm whether there are
actual claimants and these dormant payables are supported with proper
documentation; and
b. seek authority from the Sanggunian Bayan for the reversion of dormant
payables, except unliquidated balances of Accounts Payable in the Trust Fund,
to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund pursuant to Section 98 PD.
1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw
a JEV to adjust the Accounts Payable account and the Government Equity account to
present them fairly in the financial statements.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to immediately liquidate his cash advance.
Likewise, require the Sanggunian Pambayan member Roberto Flores to liquidate his
cash advance or require the refund of the same in case of non-liquidation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
monitor the liquidation of cash advances and ensure that all cash advances are fully
liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
determine the source of discrepancy and draw a JEV to adjust the loan payable
balance to fairly present the account in the financial statement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make representation with the Sanggunian
Pambayan for the post-facto creation of the SAGE for the BGCM as an addendum to
Appropriation Ordinance No. 02 in compliance with Section 3.7 of the Local Budget
Circular No. 125.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team for verification;
and
b. ensure that all documentary requirements are complied with before certifying as
to the completeness of the supporting documents of the disbursement
vouchers before payment, to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. record purchases of goods and materials as inventory and to recognize
expense upon issuance based on the SSMI; and
b. require the issuance of goods to be supported with RIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to:
a. ensure the strict compliance with the provision of EO No. 51, whereby the NFA
shall be the supplier of rice that the government entity needs in connection
with its functions unless a waiver is issued by the NFA; and
b. submit to the Audit Team the required waiver for the procurement of rice that
was not sourced from the NFA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and the OIC
- Municipal Planning and Development Office to:
a. ensure optimal implementation of projects during the year; and
b. see to it that funds are budgeted and utilized in accordance with the guidelines
on the appropriation and utilization of 20 percent DF.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer to include in the report for 20 percent DF all continuing appropriation to
enhance transparency and boost confidence in the report.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor remind the Sanggunian Pambayan
to consider authorizing any reversion of continuing appropriation and its use to other
projects through an Ordinance that is more authoritative and permanent. Likewise,
indicate in the reversion of continuing appropriation the reason/causes of reversion
52
to provide further transparency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. stop the payment of RATA to Ms. Balabala, Ms. Tan, and Mr. Acaylar; and,
b. required the refund of RATA they received without proper authorization.

Moving forward, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the rules and regulations in granting
RATA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit copies of DTRs and certification to the Audit Team to determine the
propriety of claims; and
b. require the attachment of DTR or any proof of actual rendition of service to
the payroll on the claim of RATA and desist from processing claims for
salaries and other emoluments requiring rendition of actual service early in
the month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor desist from paying grants of benefits to job
order employees and volunteers without proper authorization.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit a written justification why the
payment for EEI should not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. request for written concurrence from the Commission on Audit to forestall the
disallowance of payment to Atty. Ragaza; and
b. desist from entering into a contract for legal services without the written
concurrence of the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents to facilitate the review of
disbursements and to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit. Likewise,
direct the MGSO to register the said motor vehicles under the name of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local School Board to:
a. utilize the Special Education Fund for priority projects and purposes explicitly
allowed in the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC Nos. 1 and 2, s. 2020 in addition to JC No.
1, s. 2017; and
b. consider including in the source of revenue the unexpended balance of
previous years in the preparation of the annual budget.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor remind the Municipal Accountant to
strictly follow the description of each account in recording expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to set
up and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of operating
performance and viability of the Municipality's economic enterprises and 20%
Development Fund, and to serve as a decision-making tool of the agency. Also,
record all transactions about these economic enterprises to the corresponding
subsidiary ledgers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. ensure that supporting documents are complete before granting of benefits to
personnel; and
b. submit to the Audit Team the required documents to avoid disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. initiate the transfer of interest income earned from bank deposits under the Trust
Fund to the General Fund; and
b. monitor the interest income earned in the Trust Fund and initiate its transfer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant to remit the RPT and SET shares and other collections due to the
Provincial Government and the component Barangays within the prescribed period in
accordance with Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 92-382.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal
Accountant to prepare BRS for all depository accounts and submit them to the Auditor
for his verification within ten days after the end of each month to ascertain the reliability
of the Cash in Bank account.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with Item No. 3 of Annex D of the
Revised-IRR of RA 9184 by deducting any amount of liquidated damages from
the claim of the supplier as a result of the delay in the delivery of goods; and
b. collect the amount of P39,744.00 from Pomelio Trading representing the
amount of liquidated damages.

We further recommended the Municipal Mayor to continue sending a demand letter to the
supplier and consider imposing sanctions authorized under RA No. 9184 and its
implementing rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and
Award Committee to submit necessary documents such as:
a. Grain Business License issued by the National Food Authority; and
b. List of completed projects involving delivery of rice and conduct of rice
retailing.

We further recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to
defer processing of DV with incomplete documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the City Engineer and the Planning Officer II to submit to the Audit
Team at the beginning of the year the list of infrastructure projects and non-
infrastructure PPAs, respectively, that are ongoing and those that are to be
implemented during the year; and
b. inform the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project
signboards and/or public notices have been already posted in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC – Municipal General
Services Officer to submit the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official
Travels and ensure that the required reports are properly accomplished.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare accounting entries to take up the cancellation of stale checks in the Books of Account of the Municipality by debiting Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account and crediting Accounts Payable both in the amount of P100,036.97; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and send written notice to the payees at least one month before a check becomes stale as required under Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements for all depository accounts and submit to the Auditor for his verification within ten days after the end of each month to ascertain the reliability of the Cash in Bank.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
 cancel the official receipt covering dishonored check and to provide the Municipal Accountant with the debit memo and the photocopy of dishonored check pursuant to Section 57 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
 instruct the collector to issue an official receipt in acknowledging replacement of dishonored check as required in Section 29 of same MNGAS;

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Voucher to correct the noted deficiencies by debiting Cash  Local Treasury by P49,846.30 and crediting Prior Years Adjustment and Cash in Bank - LCCA by P12,166.30 and P37,680.00, respectively.
observations.managementAction: A memorandum was issued to Ms. Flordeliza A. Santelices, Municipal Treasurer, and Ms. Josephine G. Apalla, Municipal Accountant to address the noted deficiencies. The Municipal Accountant drew the necessary Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-2020-03-03 dated March 31, 2020 to adjust the account. Follow calls were made to payees to replace the dishonored checks and the payees replaced the dishonored checks with cash hence the official receipts was not replaced. The Municipal Accountant prepared the Bank Reconciliation Statements and submitted them to the Auditor but the Bank Reconciliation Statements were submitted beyond the reglementary period.
observations.reason: Bank Reconciliation Statements were not submitted within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to conduct physical inventory of PPE and biological assets and prepare and submit the RPCPPE; and
b. Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of PPE and biological assets and submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction: A memorandum was issued to Ms. Flordeliza S. Santelices instructing her to undertake a physical inventory of PPE and biological assets and prepare and submit the RPCPPE, as well as, inventory report on biological assets to the Office of the Auditor not later January 31 of each year. The Municipal Accountant and OIC-MGSO submitted some inventory-related documents and a Report on Physical Count Inventory dated June 30, 2020; however, the submitted reports were not the complete RPCPPE required.
observations.reason: No complete physical inventory was conducted and no RPCPPE was prepared and submitted within the prescribed period. The report submitted (June 30, 2020) was incomplete and did not contain all required data.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required information in the Notes to Financial Statements to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with the additional information value.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that she will exert effort to modify the manner the Notes to F/S is presented to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Some items in the Statement of Cash Flows and Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity were made for several financial items. A reconciliation of discrepancies between the balances presented in the SCBAA and SFPer were not shown.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to revert to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund the liability accounts totaling P207,283.72 by drawing the accounting entry debiting Accounts Payable and Due to GOCCs by P142,672.23 and P64,611.49, respectively, and crediting Prior Years Adjustments in the amount of P207,283.72.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant drew Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2020-03-001 dated March 31, 2020 to adjust the account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
 conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of receivables and determine the concerned debtors pursuant to paragraph 7.1 of COA Circular No. 2016-005;
 exert effort in determining the collectability of the receivables and set up allowance for impairment for recognition of possible losses pursuant to paragraphs 67 and 72 of IPSAS 29; and
 complete the processing of requirements on filing of request for authority to write-off dormant receivables in accordance to COA Circular No. 2016-005 and submit to the Auditor for review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: A verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of receivables as well as determination of debtors were made which resulted to the letter-request for the write-off of several receivable accounts. A letter-request for write-off was made on January 20, 2021, for the following accounts: 1) Other Receivables  Kalusugan, Kabuhayan at Kaunlaran (KKK) Receivables P31,950.00; 2) Other Receivables  Dambo-Asufre Irrigators Association P100,668.00; 3) Loans Receivables  Others P2,980,739.00. However, impairment was not set-up and the letter request lacked the required supporting documents.
observations.reason: No set-up of allowance for impairment loss was made. The letter-request for write-off lacked required supporting documents per COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of unexpended balance of Municipalitys DRRMF in the Trust Fund books upon actual disbursement of fund out of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant drew Journal Entry Voucher to record the transfer and the unexpended balance of Municipalitys DRRMF was transferred per Official Receipt No. 5681678 dated January 29, 2020 to the Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor rescind the contract due to unreasonable delay in the completion of the project, imposed appropriate sanctions against the contractor as provided in the R-IRR, and direct the:
 Municipal Treasurer to claim the performance security of the contractor from the insurance company;
 Municipal Engineer to make a recommendation to the Local Chief Executive on the rescission of contract and takeover the prosecution of the project or award the same to qualified contractor in case of delay in the completion of the work of the future infrastructure project; and
 Bids and Awards Committee to explain in writing why the contract was awarded to a contractor that has no technical experience in undertaking construction of barangay bridge under design and build scheme, and why the expenditures amounting to P983,013.30 equivalent to 15 percent project completion should not be disallowed in audit; and recommend the award of contract to qualified contractor who has technical expertise in undertaking similar contract to assure timely completion of the project.
observations.managementAction: The Management provided written justification on their actions and complied with the recommendation. The Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Award Committee to:
 submit documents and explanation in writing to the Auditor why the contract was awarded to an ineligible supplier and why the amount of P531,700.00 should not be disallowed in audit; and
 evaluate the bid documents submitted by the bidders meticulously and recommend the award of the contract to eligible and responsive bidder only.
observations.managementAction:
An explanation was submitted to the Audit Team on December 3, 2020; and evaluation of bid documents are meticulously made. However, the noted deficiency in the verification of the disbursement voucher and the supporting bid documents in the previous years AAR was not submitted for review. The noted deficiencies: 
 The authority of the supplier to engage in rice selling is doubtful since the nature of business of the supplier is general merchandising as indicated in the mayors permit, while the bid documents did not include grains business license issued by NFA being required to all persons who are engaged or are intending to engage in the rice and/or corn business under Section 1, Regulation No. II of Revised Rules and Regulations of the NFA on Grain Business; and
 The submitted list of completed projects did not contain completed contract(s) involving delivery of rice and conduct of training on rice retailing amounting to at least 50 percent of the ABC as required under Section 23.4.1.3 of the R-IRR.
observations.reason: Some required documents were not submitted; noted deficiencies in supplier eligibility remain.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 require the BAC Secretary to meticulously and judiciously plan the procurement and prepare the Municipalitys APP and submit it to the Audit Team for audit;
 direct the end-users to prepare the purchase request for goods based on the approved APP; and
 direct the Bids and Awards Committee to: a) purchase goods through public biding and employ alternative method of procurement only in highly exceptional cases as provided for in the R-IRR; b) avoid splitting of government contracts; and c) explain in writing why the expenditures for the purchase of materials should not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the recommendation. The Bids and Awards Committee provided an explanation in writing. The end-users prepared procurement request based on the APP. The Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to explain the reason/s on the non-provision of complete information and non-updating of signboards of PPAs; and henceforth ensure that the succeeding signboards of all implemented PPAs contained complete information and updated in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:

 Municipal Accountant to ensure that the payment for annual PhilHealth contribution of indigent members is supported with the list of beneficiaries and other documents showing evaluation of the MSWDO on the beneficiaries eligibility under the indigent program; and
 submit immediately to the Audit Team the lacking documents to facilitate the review of disbursements and to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the recommendation. The Municipal Accountant assured compliance and provided the required documents.

observations.recommendation:

 GAD Focal Point System to cause the posting of GAD fund utilization in conspicuous places and uploading in the DILG Regional or DILG Central Office websites in compliance with the full disclosure policy of the government.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to start the preparation and maintenance of Local Road Network Property Cards as required by the COA Circular 2015-008. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects as the latter’s reference in the maintenance of Local Road Network Property Card. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose information on the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements following the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Municipal Treasurer. No action was taken by the Municipal Engineer. The Municipal Accountant has not yet disclosed information on the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer has not yet updated the property records; The Municipal Engineer has not yet provided the Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; The Municipal Accountant has not yet disclosed the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to release the barangay’s share from RPT and CTC within five days after the end of each quarter as mandated under Section 271 (d) of LGC and Section 15 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Municipal Treasurer within five days after the end of each quarter.
observations.reason: Barangay’s share from RPT and CTC were not released to respective Barangays by the Municipal Treasurer within five days after the end of each quarter.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor address the issue on manpower
constraint to ensure that the required physical inventory of PPE is undertaken and
the RPCPPE is prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than
January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
draw a Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the non-current portion of the loans
payable to LISCOP-MDFO/World Bank from P2,451,159.97 to P1,045,735.71 by
considering the following accounting entries:
Debit Credit
Loans Payable – Domestic (current) P1,405,424.26
Loans Payable – Domestic (non-current) P1,405,424.26

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare Journal Entry Voucher and take up the following accounting entries to
correct the noted deficiencies:
Debit Credit
Due to LGUs P275,292.80
Prior Year’s Adjustment P275,292.80

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
▪ Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects; and
▪ Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property
Cards, and the Municipal Accountant to disclose the Road Networks in the
Notes to Financial Statement based on documents provided by the Municipal
Engineer containing complete information of road projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare Journal Entry Voucher and take up the following accounting entries to
correct the noted deficiencies:
Debit Credit
Receivable – Disallowance/Charges P2,500.00
Prior Year’s Adjustment P2,500.00

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
▪ General Manager of PREP to stop the practice of issuing provisionary receipts
in collecting rental fees on cottages and picnic huts, and to remit to the
Municipal Treasurer the amount of P172,040.00 representing PREP’s
Management share on rental fees for CY 2018; and
▪ Municipal Treasurer to collect all types of fees pertaining to operations of
PREP, she being the authorized official responsible for the collection of all
types of income of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
immediately submit the documents needed to support the disbursements for the
abovementioned projects.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to procure goods based on the relevant specifications or characteristics instead of specific brand name.
observations.managementAction: Procurements of goods made by the Municipality were already based on the relevant specifications or characteristics.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: • Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to require the submission of Mayor’s/Business Permit and PhilGEPS Registration for the procurement of goods through shopping as required under Annex H and Appendix A of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 before the issuance of Notice of Award by Head of the Procuring Entity; • Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the suppliers of goods which were procured through shopping to facilitate the immediate submission of the lacking documents to support the validity and propriety of the said transactions; • Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documentary requirements for the purchase of lot for the road right-of-way, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001; and • Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Engineer to immediately submit the documents needed to support the disbursements for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Procurements of goods through shopping were already submitted to the Audit Team. The required documentary requirements for the purchase of lot for the road right-of-way were already submitted to the Audit Team. The required documents for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects were already submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru its LGU GAD Focal Point System, fully utilize the GAD Budget and implement the GAD programs, projects and activities as planned.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality utilized 94 per cent of total appropriated for GAD in addition to GAD attributed programs/projects taken from the regular fund of the Municipality in a total amount of P4,647,648.13.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: ▪ Municipal Accountant to provide sufficient disclosure of the LDDRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements; and ▪ Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to submit the Report on the Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Report on the Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund was already submitted to the Audit Team monthly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to fully utilize the LCPC fund allocation to improve children’s lives and help reduce, if not eliminate, juvenile delinquency in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LCPC fund was almost fully utilized in Calendar Year 2018 with 96 per cent utilization rate.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the:

(a) Inventory Committee in close coordination with the Municipal General
Services Officer conduct complete physical count of all properties of the
Municipality;

(b) Municipal General Services Office and Municipal Accounting Office secure
relevant documents that will clarify, substantiate and establish pertinent
information leading to the completeness of the inventory reports and furnish
the Auditor with a copy of the reports within the time frame provided by
regulations.

(¢) General Services Officer update all property records and regularly reconcile
them with the accounting records to ensure the accuracy of the PPE
balances reported in the Statement of Financial Position as of December 31,
2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to adopt the Perpetual
Inventory Method in accounting for inventory items.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
General Services Officer to start the preparation and maintenance of Local
Road Network Property Card as required by the COA Circular 2015-008 dated
November 25, 2015,

In addition, we also recommended that the Municipal Accountant
disclose information on the total road network system in the Notes to Financial
Statements following the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008,

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and maintain complete subsidiary records of each
special account. Accordingly, at the end of each year, prepare trial balance and
financial statements of each special account in compliance with Section 110 of
the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate
liquidation by concerned Municipal employees of the outstanding cash advances
totaling P653,808.18 in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 97-002.
Henceforth, see to it that all outstanding cash advances are fully liquidated at
the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all the concerned
officials to explain/justify the reason/s why expenditures amounting to
£29.960.00 were charged against the Special Education Fund otherwise, the
whole amount should be refunded.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local School
Board Officials to strictly utilize the Special Education Fund for purposes
provided under Section 1 of RA No. 5447, Sections 272 and 100 RA No. 7160
and Joint Circular Nos. 01, 01A and 01-B of DECS-DBM-DILG.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
General Services Officer and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman to
furnish the Auditor with copies of contracts and purchase orders within the period
prescribed under COA Circular No. 87-278 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027,
as restated and updated under COA Circular No. 2009001, for the timely review
of contracts and purchase orders and conduct of inspection of deliveries and
infrastructure projects,

We also recommended immediate submission of the required supporting documents
and notice of deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance of the items.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require
the Municipal Accountant: a) to set-up Real Property Tax Receivable and
Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year based on duly
certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which
should be provided by the Municipal Treasurer; b) credit the Real Property
Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts upon collection
of real property and special education taxes, respectively; and c) analyze the
discrepancy in reporting the balances of Real Property and Special Education
Tax receivable, afterwhich, draw a journal entry voucher to take up the
necessary adjustments.
